claude.output_text:
```json
{"findings":[
  {"finding_id":"F1","severity":"critical","category":"bug","title":"DAG execution conflates stage-level graph with work-unit-level graph","evidence":{"file":"plans/swarm-do-1.12-orchestration-friction-fixes.md","line":316,"symbol":"1.12c DoD","snippet":"Pipeline engine consumes the DAG: independent work units run in parallel worktrees via the existing Agent/Task infrastructure."},"recommendation":"The current engine (swarm-do/py/swarm_do/pipeline/engine.py:21-34) only topologically orders stages; it has no per-work-unit graph axis, and fan_out produces sibling agents that share one merge node (not a unit-level DAG). The plan must specify whether work units become stages (requiring dynamic stage emission), a new `work_units` construct, or an extension of fan_out with inter-branch `depends_on`. Without this choice, 'pipeline engine consumes the DAG' is not implementable against the existing engine, which the SKILL.md even forbids extending with `parallel_with` (swarm-do/skills/swarm-do/SKILL.md:95).","confidence":0.95,"fingerprint":"1.12c-dag-axis-undefined"},
  {"finding_id":"F2","severity":"high","category":"bug","title":"`pipeline.parallelism` key will be rejected by the existing pipeline schema lint","evidence":{"file":"swarm-do/py/swarm_do/pipeline/validation.py","line":18,"symbol":"PIPELINE_TOP_KEYS","snippet":"PIPELINE_TOP_KEYS = {\"pipeline_version\", \"name\", \"description\", \"stages\"}"},"recommendation":"1.12c DoD references `pipeline.parallelism: 1` as the serial fallback toggle (plans/swarm-do-1.12-orchestration-friction-fixes.md:318) but schema_lint_pipeline emits 'unknown top-level keys' for anything outside PIPELINE_TOP_KEYS (validation.py:106-108). Add `parallelism` to the allowed set, specify whether it lives in pipeline YAML or preset.budget, and state whether serial mode applies at stage layers, work-unit layers, or both.","confidence":0.98,"fingerprint":"1.12c-parallelism-key-unlinted"},
  {"finding_id":"F3","severity":"critical","category":"bug","title":"Per-work-unit merge-to-integration-branch has no conflict/rollback contract","evidence":{"file":"plans/swarm-do-1.12-orchestration-friction-fixes.md","line":322,"symbol":"1.12c per-work-unit commit","snippet":"Per-work-unit commit: writer's DoD includes a commit on its worktree branch before spec-review runs. Orchestrator merges to integration branch after spec-review APPROVED."},"recommendation":"Merge safety is undefined for: (a) two parallel units touching the same file (rebase/merge strategy, conflict resolution policy), (b) integration branch lifecycle (who creates/names/cleans it, fail cleanup), (c) rollback when orchestrator-side independent validation (1.12d) fails after the writer already committed, and (d) how `bd sync` races across concurrent worktrees that each write to `.beads/`. Without these, 'parallel worktrees' is a merge-corruption risk on first real fan-out.","confidence":0.9,"fingerprint":"1.12c-integration-branch-unspecified"},
  {"finding_id":"F4","severity":"high","category":"bug","title":"Retry-cap state machine is ambiguous on non-SPEC_MISMATCH verdicts and retry persistence","evidence":{"file":"plans/swarm-do-1.12-orchestration-friction-fixes.md","line":320,"symbol":"1.12c retry cap","snippet":"Spec-review rejection respawns writer with review notes, max 2 retries, then escalates (matches metaswarm's 3-iteration cap)."},"recommendation":"agent-spec-review emits three verdicts (APPROVED, SPEC_MISMATCH, SPEC_AMBIGUOUS — swarm-do/roles/agent-spec-review/shared.md:57) and writer emits four status values including BLOCKED/NEEDS_CONTEXT (roles/agent-writer/shared.md:65-75). Plan does not say whether SPEC_AMBIGUOUS counts against the cap, whether BLOCKED/NEEDS_CONTEXT on retry N counts, how the retry counter survives PreCompact/context overflow (1.12a talks about checkpoint artifacts but retry-state schema is not listed), or what `escalates to operator` looks like as a BEADS state transition. Define the state machine explicitly: states, transitions, counter persistence location, escalation verdict.","confidence":0.92,"fingerprint":"1.12c-retry-state-machine-gaps"},
  {"finding_id":"F5","severity":"high","category":"bug","title":"Writer role lacks a 'respawn with review feedback' input contract","evidence":{"file":"swarm-do/roles/agent-writer/shared.md","line":28,"symbol":"Sequencing & ownership","snippet":"Read this issue. Read upstream analysis notes (or debug notes if `kind: bug`). Read clarify notes if present."},"recommendation":"1.12c retry semantics require the orchestrator to 'respawn writer with review notes', but the writer persona has no spec for a spec-review-feedback input channel or for prioritizing it over the original work breakdown. 1.12c DoD must list role-prompt updates to agent-writer (sequencing, grounding rules for rework, and output-format changes) — the current 1.12c scope is engine-only and will produce an incoherent writer on retry.","confidence":0.9,"fingerprint":"1.12c-writer-no-retry-contract"},
  {"finding_id":"F6","severity":"high","category":"bug","title":"1.12c retry cap depends on file:line evidence schema scheduled for 1.12d","evidence":{"file":"plans/swarm-do-1.12-orchestration-friction-fixes.md","line":336,"symbol":"1.12d adversarial contract","snippet":"Schema validation on output; rejections missing evidence are auto-discarded by the orchestrator and retried once."},"recommendation":"1.12c triggers respawn on SPEC_MISMATCH, but the spec-review output is still free-form markdown (swarm-do/roles/agent-spec-review/shared.md:54-73). Without the evidence schema and parser shipped in 1.12d, the orchestrator cannot programmatically detect SPEC_MISMATCH vs pass, cannot enforce file:line presence, and cannot count retries reliably. Either promote the schema/parser into 1.12c or declare a blocking dependency so 1.12d must land first.","confidence":0.9,"fingerprint":"1.12c-missing-verdict-schema"},
  {"finding_id":"F7","severity":"high","category":"bug","title":"BEADS concurrent writes across parallel worktrees not addressed","evidence":{"file":"plans/swarm-do-1.12-orchestration-friction-fixes.md","line":318,"symbol":"DAG execution","snippet":"independent work units run in parallel worktrees via the existing Agent/Task infrastructure"},"recommendation":"The plan mandates BEADS as the authoritative resume store (plans/swarm-do-1.12-orchestration-friction-fixes.md:354) and relies on git-backed `.beads/` state. Parallel worktrees each running `bd update` + `bd sync` will race on the same `.beads/` files and produce merge conflicts or lost notes. Specify a single-writer discipline for BEADS (e.g., only the orchestrator writes; writers emit structured notes the orchestrator appends) or document conflict resolution. This is a concrete merge-safety blocker.","confidence":0.85,"fingerprint":"1.12c-beads-concurrency"},
  {"finding_id":"F8","severity":"medium","category":"bug","title":"Orchestrator independent-validation ordering inverts the per-unit commit contract","evidence":{"file":"plans/swarm-do-1.12-orchestration-friction-fixes.md","line":322,"symbol":"1.12c commit ordering","snippet":"writer's DoD includes a commit on its worktree branch before spec-review runs"},"recommendation":"1.12d says the orchestrator runs pytest/ruff 'independently after each writer phase' and feeds objective failures into spec-review. That means the writer may commit based on its own green tests, orchestrator re-runs and finds red, spec-review rejects, retry loops — but the bad commit is already on the worktree branch. Specify whether the writer amends (conflicts with agent-writer/shared.md:43 'Do not amend previous commits'), resets, or whether commit is deferred until orchestrator validation passes.","confidence":0.85,"fingerprint":"1.12c-validation-commit-order"},
  {"finding_id":"F9","severity":"medium","category":"maintainability","title":"Work-unit artifact schema is not specified","evidence":{"file":"plans/swarm-do-1.12-orchestration-friction-fixes.md","line":315,"symbol":"decompose-plan emission","snippet":"`decompose-plan` stage (from 1.11 superpowers plan) extended to emit `depends_on` edges as first-class data on the work-unit artifact."},"recommendation":"No schema version, field list, or storage location is defined for the work-unit artifact. The existing codebase has versioned telemetry schemas (runs.v1, findings.v2 — SKILL.md and plan section 'swarm-do current state'). Ship `work_units.v1` with id, depends_on, files, acceptance_criteria, beads_id, worktree_branch, retry_count before 1.12c can be executed. Also specify cycle detection at the work-unit layer — engine.py:29-31 only guards stage-level cycles.","confidence":0.85,"fingerprint":"1.12c-workunit-schema-missing"},
  {"finding_id":"F10","severity":"medium","category":"maintainability","title":"1.12c omits dispatcher/SKILL.md changes required for integration-branch merging","evidence":{"file":"swarm-do/skills/swarm-do/SKILL.md","line":52,"symbol":"Dispatch Loop","snippet":"Load the active pipeline via `bin/swarm pipeline show <name>` and follow its topological layers."},"recommendation":"The dispatch loop has no concept of worktree creation, integration branch, per-unit commit, or merge step. 1.12c DoD enumerates engine work and role work but no SKILL.md dispatcher work. Without that, the deterministic engine can emit a DAG the dispatcher does not know how to execute. Add an explicit sub-task: update SKILL.md with worktree-branch lifecycle, merge-on-APPROVED step, conflict-on-merge handling, and retry dispatch.","confidence":0.88,"fingerprint":"1.12c-skill-dispatcher-gap"},
  {"finding_id":"F11","severity":"medium","category":"bug","title":"Promotion scorecard 'SPEC_MISMATCH retry rate < 25%' presupposes telemetry not listed in DoD","evidence":{"file":"plans/swarm-do-1.12-orchestration-friction-fixes.md","line":377,"symbol":"1.12c promote if","snippet":"SPEC_MISMATCH retry rate < 25%; no increase in merge conflicts."},"recommendation":"runs.v1 is frozen (1.12a note, line 295). 1.12c must ship either runs.v2 or a companion events stream carrying `spec_mismatch_retry`, `retry_exhausted_escalation`, and `worktree_merge_conflict` events so the promotion gate is measurable. DoD does not list this telemetry; the scorecard cannot be evaluated as written.","confidence":0.82,"fingerprint":"1.12c-telemetry-for-scorecard"}
]}
```
